threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think a 10-minute wait for a bus is a crisis. Meanwhile, the locals are casually 20 minutes late to everything, no one’s phased.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 90s R&B. By the time we hit Tulse Hill, we were all clapping along, totally forgetting about the strikes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day the Turkish bakery down the street opened at 5 AM, and the smell of fresh simit woke me up before sunrise. That's when it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find spontaneous silent discos under the Oberbaum Bridge, where everyone dances while wearing headphones, lost in their own world.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you instinctively duck into a taqueria when the BART's delayed, instead of standing there looking confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 at Civic Center today, and a guy was rapping his way through the whole ride. Only in SF can a bus feel like a live show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' is just about the parks, but it’s really the casual chats in the plazas that define us. That’s where life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they shy away from ordering tapas without knowing the 'just one' rule—grabbing plates makes them nervous.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still treat the bus as a last resort, and end up staring at the Tube map like it's hieroglyphics. Welcome to the chaos!
Last night on the 59, a bloke serenaded the bus with a karaoke version of 'No Woman, No Cry'. We all just sat back, half-laughing, half-cringing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade on the street, and the whole block stopped to chat and sip. Only here, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a late-night kebab stand that doubles as an art gallery. Grab your döner while critiquing local art on the walls.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they instinctively avoid the sidewalk 'art' made by the pigeons near Dolores. Tourists always stop to snap pics.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni delay at Civic Center today because a dog decided to take a nap on the tracks. Only in SF do we have canine traffic jams.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' is just for partying. They miss the small, hidden art galleries where locals quietly showcase their work.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones marveling at the colorful mosaics in Carrer de les afores, snapping photos like it's a hidden gem—meanwhile, locals just walk by.
